info.title: Antithesis API
info.description: The Antithesis API enables programmatic access to the Antithesis autonomous testing environments. List runs, inspect run details and retrieve logs.

tags.name: Launch
tags.description: Launch test runs and debugging sessions

get.description: Returns a paginated list of runs for your organization. Results are ordered by creation time, newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Server-capped at 100.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Pass the `next_cursor` value (as well as any filter parameters) from a previous response to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Only return runs created after this timestamp (ISO 8601).

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Only return runs created before this timestamp (ISO 8601).

parameters.name: launcher
parameters.description: Filter by the launcher that kicked off the run.

get.description: Returns the full details of a single run, including results.

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the run to retrieve.

200.description: Full details of the requested run.

mvd_run.summary: A multiverse debugging session with debugger ready

post.description: Kicks off a test using the specified launcher (e.g. `basic_test` or `basic_k8s_test`). Fetches the relevant container images specified in configuration files and in `antithesis.images`, builds the test environment, and runs the test for the duration set in `antithesis.duration`. Returns an HTTP status code indicating whether the test was successfully started (not the test result itself).

parameters.name: launcher_name
parameters.description: Name of the launcher to use. Use `basic_test` for Docker Compose orchestration or `basic_k8s_test` for Kubernetes orchestration.

Error_Response.description: Standard error envelope returned by all error responses.

message.description: A human-readable explanation of what went wrong.

Run_List_Response.description: A page of test run summaries returned by the list endpoint. Use `next_cursor` to iterate through all available results.

next_cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor. Pass as the `after` query parameter to fetch the next page. Null if no more pages.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the run was created (ISO 8601).

started_at.description: Timestamp when the run started executing. Null if the run has not yet started.

completed_at.description: Timestamp when the run finished. Null if the run is still in progress or has not started.

launcher.description: The name of the launcher that kicked off this run.

Run_Detail.description: Full details of a test run. Extends Run_Summary with session information, results, and environment metadata.

session_id.description: Unique identifier for the test session. Used to correlate logs, events, and search queries back to this specific execution.

results.description: Aggregate results for the run. Structure varies by run type. May be absent or partial for runs that have not yet completed.

Launch_Response.description: Response returned when a test is successfully launched.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for the launched run.

Launch_Request.description: Request body for launching a test.

Params.description: Key-value pairs of run parameters. Used both when launching a test and when inspecting a run's configuration. All properties are optional.

antithesis.config_image.description: The image version of your config image. Should be a single image version formatted as `image_name:tag`.

antithesis.description.description: A description of the test run. Appears in report headers and any emails triggered by the run.

antithesis.duration.description: Desired duration of the test run in minutes.

antithesis.images.description: A semicolon-delimited list of container image versions needed in the testing environment. Each entry in the format `[REGISTRY/]NAME(:TAG|@DIGEST)`. Images specified by digest will be tagged as `latest` within the Antithesis environment.

antithesis.is_ephemeral.description: Whether the run's results should be excluded from historic reports. Set to `"true"` for one-off exploratory runs. Defaults to `"false"`.

antithesis.report.recipients.description: A semicolon-delimited list of email addresses to receive the triage report link. If not specified, emails are sent to the default users configured for the endpoint.

antithesis.source.description: An identifier used to separate property history in reports. Each property's history is generated from all previous runs sharing the same source value. Useful for tracking test history per branch.

additionalProperties.description: Additional parameters including tooling integration parameters (e.g. `antithesis.integrations.<type>.callback_url`, `antithesis.integrations.<type>.token` where type is `github`, `discord`, or `slack`).

Run_Status.description: Current lifecycle status of a run. `starting` — the run is being set up. `in_progress` — the run is actively running. `debugger_ready` — the multiverse debugger (MVD) session is ready for interaction (MVD runs only). `completed` — the run finished successfully. `cancelled` — the run was cancelled before completion. `failed` — the run did not complete successfully (incomplete).

Run_Environment.description: Environment details for the test run, including the Antithesis platform version and the full set of container images that were deployed.

antithesis_version.description: Version of the Antithesis platform used for this run.

images.description: Container images deployed in this run.

Container_Image.description: A container image used in the test environment.

name.description: Logical name of the service or component.

image.description: Docker image digest, a content-addressable identifier for the image.

tag.description: Image tag identifying the specific version.

Run_Type.description: The type of run. `test` is a standard test run, `mvd` is a multiverse debugging session, `other` covers any additional run types.

Creator_Info.description: Information about who or what created this run. Distinguishes between CI-triggered runs, manual user-initiated runs, and agent-driven runs.

Run_Links.description: Relevant links for this run, providing direct access to the triage report.

triage_report.description: URL to the triage report for this run.

BadRequest.description: Bad request — likely missing or invalid parameters.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized — invalid or missing authentication credentials.

NotFound.description: The requested resource was not found.

TooManyRequests.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check standard rate-limit headers (Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Remaining, etc.) and back off.

example.message: Too many requests. Retry after 30 seconds.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

BearerAuth.description: API key provided by your Antithesis forward deployment engineer. Pass the key in the Authorization header as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`.
